root: ء ه ع ح غ خ

entries.definition:
(although the ع and غ are in fact uvular sounds and
not guttural) as in (108:2) وانحر (recited as wanhar); (96:2) مِنْ عَلَق
(recited as min ʿalaq); (112:4) كُفُوًا أَحَدٌ (recited as
kufuwan ʾaḥad) 2 a) complete assimilation with nasalisation
(إدغام بغنة) of the nūn or the nunation into the second consonant of
the cluster in cases where that consonant is one of: م/ي/و/ن and
only where the cluster occurs between two words as in (79:8) قُلُوبٌ
يَوْمَئِذٍ (qulu:bũyyawma ʔiðin); (77:20) مِنْ مَاءٍ مَهِينٍ (min māʾin mahīn
recited as mimmaʔim-mahi:n). Complete assimilation does not occur within single
words for it would obscure the identity of the words, cf. (87:16)

911

النون

الدُّنْيَا (recited as ʔad-dunyā:) b) complete assimilation without
nasalisation (إدغام بغير غنة) into the second consonant in the cluster
where that consonant is either ل or ر (this rule is represented in
the printed text of the Qur’an by the shadda (ـّ)) in (96:7) أَنْ رَآهُ
(ʾan raʾāhu recited as ʔarraʔa:hu); (18:6) إِنْ لَمْ يُؤْمِنُوا (ʾin lam
yuʾminū recited as ʔillamyuʔminu:) 3 transformation (إقلاب) that
is the changing of the sound of the nūn (ن) into a mīm (م), a
bi-labial nasal, in cases where the second consonant in the cluster
is a bāʾ (ب), a voiced bi-labial plosive, as in (30:4) وَمِنْ بَعْدِ
(wa min baʿdi recited as wa-mimbɑʕd) and (2:19) مُحِيطٌ بِالْكَافِرِينَ
(muḥīṭun biʾl-kāfirīn recited as muḥi:tumbil-ka:fi:ri:n) 4 concealment
(إخفاء) which could be chracterised as a partial assimilation of the
nūn, in cases where the second member of the cluster is one of
fifteen consonantal sounds, with each of which the nūn is
differently realised (while the second consonant in the cluster
keeps its full identity). The nūn is realised in these cases
differently as follows: uvular-nasal with ق as in (4:17) مِنْ قَرِيبٍ
(min qarīb recited as miŋqari:b); velar-nasal with ك as in (73:12)
أَنْكَالًا (ʾankālā recited as ʔaŋka:la); palato-alveolar nasal with ج as
in (12:96) أَنْ جَاءَ (ʾan jāʾa recited as ʔaɲja:?a); alveo-palatal
nasal with ش as in (6:41) إِنْ شَاءَ (ʾin shāʾa recited as ʔiɲ+ʃa:?a);
alveolar nasal with س، ز and ص as in (33:23) رِجَالٌ صَدَقُوا (rijālun
ṣadaqū recited as rija:lũn-ṣadaqu:); dento-alveolar nasal with د،
ت، ض and ط as in (4:4) فَإِنْ طِبْنَ (fa-ʾin ṭibna recited as
fa-ʔinṭibna); dental-nasal with ذ، ث and ظ as in (111:3) نَارًا ذَاتَ
لَهَبٍ (nāran dhāta lahab recited as na:ran+ða:ta lahab) and
labio-dental nasal with ف as in (8:1) الْأَنْفَالِ (ʾal-ʾanfāl recited as
ʔal-ɱfa:l). In the printed text of the Qur’an, to which the
reader is referred, the doubled signs of فتحة (ــً), كسرة (ــٍ) and ضمة
(ــٌ) indicating تنوين are aligned on top of one another and placed
directly above or under the letter concerned in the case of distinct
pronunciation (إظهار) as in (6:37) قَادِرٌ عَلَى; (112:4) أَحَدٌ; (28:88)
كُلُّ شَيْءٍ هَالِكٌ but in the case of assimilation (إدغام) and concealment
(إخفاء) the two signs are not aligned over one another as in (14:32)
رِزْقًا لَكُمْ; (56:89) فَرَوْحٌ وَرَيْحَانٌ; (59:13) رَهْبَةً فِي صُدُورِهِمْ;
(48:29) رُكَّعًا سُجَّدًا. In the case of transformation (إقلاب) the second sign is drawn
as a small ميم and placed either above or after the letter concerned

912

ن

as in (96:15) لَنَسْفَعًا بِالنَّاصِيَةِ; (2:19) مُحِيطٌ بِالْكَافِرِينَ; (2:41) كَافِرٌ بِهِ. The
nūn with no vowel following it (النون الساكنة), together with
nunation (التنوين), (see ن -n) and elongation of vowels (المدود),
receive particular attention in the discipline of Qur’anic
Recitation, tajwīd (تجويد).

ن -n suffix functioning as: I nūn of emphasis (نون التوكيد) of which
there are two types: 1 light nūn of emphasis (نون التوكيد الخفيفة),
suffixed to an imperfect verb and traditionally written in Qur’anic
text as ʾalif, as in (96:15) كَلَّا لَئِنْ لَمْ يَنْتَهِ لَنَسْفَعًا بِالنَّاصِيَةِ no indeed!–if he
does not desist, We shall seize him by the forelock 2 heavy nūn of
emphasis (نون التوكيد الثقيلة), also suffixed to an imperfect verb, but
written as ـنّ. It differs, as the name implies, in the degree of
emphasis it denotes, and occurs, as a rule, in conjunction with an
oath, as in (27:49) تَقَاسَمُوا بِاللَّهِ لَنُبَيِّتَنَّهُ وَأَهْلَهُ swear to one another by
God–we shall attack him and his household in the night II
nunation (تنوين) (i.e. -un, -an and -in) suffixed to a noun and
functioning, among other things, as one of the following: 1
nunation of indefiniteness (تنوين التنكير), ‘any’, as in (2:221) وَلَعَبْدٌ
مُؤْمِنٌ خَيْرٌ مِنْ مُشْرِكٍ any believing slave is certainly better than a
polytheist 2 nunation of definiteness (تنوين التعريف), ‘this particular
one’, as in (3:144) وَمَا مُحَمَّدٌ إِلَّا رَسُولٌ Muḥammad is only a
messenger 3 nunation of compensation (تنوين العوض), indicating an
elided part which is otherwise understood from the preceding
discourse. The elided part may be a) a single sound, as in (89:1–
2) وَالْفَجْرِ وَلَيَالٍ عَشْرٍ by the Daybreak, by [the] Ten Nights (instead of
ليالي) b) one word, as in (36:40) وَكُلٌّ فِي فَلَكٍ يَسْبَحُونَ each [one] floats
in an orbit (instead of كل واحد) c) a whole sentence, as in
(56:84) وَأَنْتُمْ حِينَئِذٍ تَنْظُرُونَ and you at that moment [when the spirit
reaches the gullet] gaze on (instead of حين بلغت الروح الحلقوم تنظرون)
d) or indicating several elided sentences, as in (99:4) يَوْمَئِذٍ تُحَدِّثُ
أَخْبَارَهَا on that Day [when the earth quakes and spews out its
burdens and man cries out, ‘What is wrong with it!?’] it will tell
all (instead of يوم إذا زلزلت الأرض زلزالها و أخرجت الأرض أثقالها و قال
الإنسان مالها تحدث أخبارها) (see also: يَوْمَئِذٍ yawmaʾidhin) III protective
nūn (نون الوقاية), as designated by the grammarians. It is infixed
between a verb and a following 1st pers. sing. suffix, as in (36:22)

913
